summary: Soundboard capture with a bit of background hiss, but an overall solid performance highlighted by Pigpen’s Turn On Your Lovelight and a standout acoustic jam on Mountains of the Moon.
review: The show is remembered fondly for its festival atmosphere, fluid Jerry Garcia solos and a memorable acoustic ending to “Mountains of the Moon.”  The soundboard source is generally clear, though a faint hiss can be heard; most listeners find it listenable and even recommend EQ tweaks.  Pigpen’s presence and “Turn On Your Lovelight” make this a must‑have for fans.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 5‑star, praising the performance (Pigpen, Jerry’s solos, Mountains of the Moon) while recording comments are mixed but overall acceptable; weighted combination yields a high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Background hiss makes some instruments and vocals less clear; one reviewer notes it sounds better than other copies and can be improved with EQ, but overall the soundboard is listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans recall a short, festival‑style set with high energy, fluid Jerry solos and strong Pigpen moments; the concert felt like an original 1968 rock fest with seamless transitions between bands.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Great acoustic guitar jam at the end of “Mountains of the Moon” and fluid SG‑style solos noted by several reviewers.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work on “Mountains of the Moon” is praised as solid.
